1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Agent Documentation 5→ 6→Before starting work, scan the read-when conditions below. 7→If your current task matches, read the linked document **before writing code**. 8→ 9→## Categories 10→ 11→- [architecture/](architecture/) — Explore when working on core patterns (dry-run, gateways, subprocess, shell integration). session.assistant: Now let me check what files exist in the commands directory. I see there are several files, but I don't see "skill-patterns.md" specifically. Let me read the index to understand the structure better:

1→<!-- AUTO-GENERATED FILE - DO NOT EDIT DIRECTLY --> 2→<!-- Edit source frontmatter, then run 'erk docs sync' to regenerate. --> 3→ 4→# Commands Documentation 5→ 6→- **[audit-doc.md](audit-doc.md)** — understanding why audit-doc works the way it does, modifying the /local:audit-doc command, understanding collateral finding tiers, debugging unexpected audit verdicts 7→- **[command-rename-pattern.md](command-rename-pattern.md)** — renaming a slash command or skill, migrating command invocations across the codebase, performing a terminology shift that affects command names 8→- **[optimization-patterns.md](optimization-patterns.md)** — reducing command file size, using @ reference in commands, modularizing command content 9→- **[preview-command-pattern.md](preview-command-pattern.md)** — pairing destructive commands with preview commands, implementing dry-run for slash commands, adding preview functionality to existing commands 10→- **[session-id-substitution.md](session-id-substitution.md)** — writing slash commands or skills that need session context, developing hooks that interact with Claude sessions, debugging session ID unavailable or empty string errors, deciding where to place session-dependent logic (root agent vs sub-agent) 11→- **[step-renumbering-checklist.md](step-renumbering-checklist.md)** — merging, removing, or reordering steps in slash commands, refactoring command workflows that use numbered steps, encountering broken step references after editing a command 12→- **[tool-restriction-safety.md](tool-restriction-safety.md)** — adding allowed-tools to a command or agent frontmatter, designing a read-only slash command, creating commands intended for use within plan mode, deciding which tools a restricted command needs 13→
<system-reminder> Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior. </system-reminder>

## Summary
Based on my investigation of the documentation:
**Does `docs/learned/commands/skill-patterns.md` exist?** No, the file `skill-patterns.md` does not exist in the `docs/learned/commands/` directory.
**Files in `docs/learned/commands/` directory:**
1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/commands/audit-doc.md` 2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/commands/optimization-patterns.md` 3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/commands/index.md` 4. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/commands/command-rename-pattern.md` 5. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/commands/tripwires.md` 6. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/commands/tool-restriction-safety.md` 7. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/commands/preview-command-pattern.md` 8. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/commands/session-id-substitution.md` 9. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/docs/learned/commands/step-renumbering-checklist.md`
**Related Documentation:** The `commands/` directory focuses on slash command authoring patterns. The closest existing documentation is: - `optimization-patterns.md` — covers using @ references in commands and modularizing command content - `preview-command-pattern.md` — covers pairing commands with preview functionality - `tool-restriction-safety.md` — covers adding allowed-tools and designing restricted commands
There is no dedicated `skill-patterns.md` document in this directory currently.
